title: prunus × lannesiana f. benitoranowo
intro: Prunus × lannesiana f. benitoranowo is a flowering plant species, commonly known as the Benito Tree or Benito Rose. It is native to the Philippines and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as wound healing, antimicrobial properties, and potential anti-cancer effects due to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations from the plant, such as infusions and extracts, have been traditionally used in folk medicine in the Philippines, and further research is needed to fully explore its potential uses and applications.
